title: vernonia fasciculata subsp. corymbosa
intro: Vernonia fasciculata subsp. corymbosa, also known as the Texas ironweed, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the sunflower family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and swelling, as well as protect against cell damage. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and supporting immune function. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and it can also be used in topical applications, such as salves and poultices, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
